h1.purpose: Case study headline only. Distinguished from body text by scale, not typeface.

headline.purpose: Section headers within a case study.

label.purpose: Buttons, chips, nav labels, section tags.

mono.purpose: Code, terminal UI, custom cursor labels only.

shadows.name: card-rest
shadows.purpose: Default ambient lift under homepage cards and media panels.

shadows.name: card-hover
shadows.purpose: Slightly deeper lift on card hover, still under 5% opacity.

shadows.name: chip-ambient
shadows.purpose: Near-invisible depth under pill-shaped interactive chips.

shadows.name: focus-ring
shadows.purpose: The one visible shadow in the system, reserved for accessibility focus states.

shadows.name: spotify-hover-ring
shadows.purpose: Hover ring on the homepage 'currently playing' widget's album art — the system's only interaction-accent color.

components.name: Primary Button (Liquid Fill)
components.description: Pill-shaped CTA with an ink fill that rises from the bottom on hover via spring physics, label flips to off-white.
components.html: View case study
components.css: .ds-liquid-btn { position: relative; display: inline-flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; overflow: hidden; border-radius: 9999px; border: 1px solid #111111; padding: 10px 20px; background: rgba(253,253,253,0.35); font-family: Figtree, sans-serif; font-size: 14px; font-weight: 600; letter-spacing: 0.01em; color: #111111; text-decoration: none; transition: transform 0.2s cubic-bezier(0.23,1,0.32,1); } .ds-liquid-btn:hover { transform: scale(1.05); } .ds-liquid-btn:active { transform: scale(0.95); } .ds-liquid-btn:focus-visible { outline: none; box-shadow: 0 0 0 2px #111111, 0 0 0 4px #FAFAFA; } .ds-liquid-btn__fill { position: absolute; inset: 0; background: #111111; transform: scaleY(0); transform-origin: bottom; transition: transform 0.42s cubic-bezier(0.23,1,0.32,1); z-index: 0; } .ds-liquid-btn:hover .ds-liquid-btn__fill { transform: scaleY(1); } .ds-liquid-btn__label { position: relative; z-index: 1; transition: color 0.3s; } .ds-liquid-btn:hover .ds-liquid-btn__label { color: #FDFDFD; }

components.name: Spotify Hover Ring
components.description: Currently-playing widget's album art. A hairline white border at rest; on hover, a terminal-pink ring appears around it — the system's only interaction-accent color, used nowhere else.
components.css: .ds-spotify-art { width: 88px; height: 88px; border: 1.6px solid #fff; border-radius: 8px; overflow: hidden; box-shadow: 0px 8px 22px rgba(0,0,0,0.14); transition: box-shadow 0.2s ease; } .ds-spotify-art:hover { box-shadow: 0px 8px 22px rgba(0,0,0,0.14), 0 0 0 1.5px #fff, 0 0 0 3px #d6336c; }

components.name: Media Panel
components.description: Large rounded fill used to frame case study screenshots and hero videos.
components.css: .ds-media-panel { background: #f5f5f5; border-radius: 24px; overflow: hidden; box-shadow: 0px 4px 12px 0px rgba(0,0,0,0.05); transition: box-shadow 250ms cubic-bezier(0.3,0.6,0.32,1), transform 250ms cubic-bezier(0.3,0.6,0.32,1); } .ds-media-panel:hover { box-shadow: 0px 15px 30px -22px rgba(0,0,0,0.05); }

components.name: Nav Shell
components.description: Floating full-width pill nav, glass-tinted on the homepage, transparent elsewhere, with tilt-on-hover icon links.
components.css: .ds-nav-shell { display: flex; align-items: center; justify-content: space-between; height: 60px; border-radius: 9999px; background: rgba(255,255,255,0.15); padding: 6px 12px; } .ds-nav-shell__icon { display: inline-flex; width: 32px; height: 32px; align-items: center; justify-content: center; color: #555555; transition: transform 400ms cubic-bezier(0.34,1.56,0.64,1), opacity 200ms; } .ds-nav-shell__icon:hover { transform: rotate(8deg); opacity: 0.75; }

components.name: Tag Chip
components.description: Small rounded label used for case study hashtags and metadata pills.
components.css: .ds-tag-chip { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; padding: 6px 14px; border-radius: 14px; background: #f3f5f6; color: #555555; font-family: Figtree, sans-serif; font-size: 14px; font-weight: 600; }

narrative.overview: The base surface is a workbench, not a showroom: quiet grayscale ink (#111111) on off-white (#fafafa), with no color of its own. Color shows up only when a specific piece of work is on the table — Rocket Mortgage's red, TARS's purple, Salesforce's navy — and when it shows up, it commits to that one context fully rather than bleeding into the chrome around it. This mirrors the portfolio's own thesis: the site is a work sample, not a brand exercise, so the visual system stays out of the way until a specific project earns its color. The system explicitly rejects the generic template look (interchangeable SaaS-landing gradients and stock card grids), the slow, effects-heavy feel of the site's previous Framer build, and any visual cue that reads as filler rather than considered craft. Motion and color are earned, not decorative.
keyCharacteristics: Grayscale-first: text and chrome live entirely in ink/charcoal/off-white; color is contextual, not ambient.
One committed accent per surface: each case study or interactive moment picks a single color and uses it with intent.
Paper-thin depth: shadows are felt, not seen.
One type voice: Figtree carries headlines, body, and labels; DM Mono steps in only for code and terminal contexts.
Motion favors physical fill and spring response over fades and gradients.

rules.name: The Borrowed Color Rule
rules.body: The base UI (nav, footer, global chrome) never carries a project's brand color. A color only appears inside the boundary of the case study, section, or component it belongs to, and it commits there rather than being used sparingly — one accent per context, not several diluted ones.

rules.name: The One Ink Rule
rules.body: There is exactly one near-black across the whole system (#111111). Don't introduce a second 'almost black' for a new component; reuse ink.

rules.name: The One Voice Rule
rules.body: Figtree is the only load-bearing typeface across the system — the case study H1 is Figtree at scale, not a different face. DM Mono is a second voice reserved strictly for literal code/terminal/cursor contexts — never decorative.

rules.name: The Paper-Thin Rule
rules.body: If a shadow reads as 'a shadow' rather than 'a piece of paper barely lifted,' it's too strong. Opacity stays at or below 5% except for focus rings, which exist to be seen.

dos: Do keep the base chrome (nav, footer, global page background) strictly grayscale — ink, charcoal, off-white only.
Do let exactly one borrowed color own each case study or section, applied with commitment rather than a single timid swatch.
Do keep Figtree as the one load-bearing voice across headlines, body, and labels; reserve DM Mono strictly for code and terminal contexts.
Do keep shadows at or below 5% opacity at rest; let motion carry interactivity instead of heavier shadows or color swaps.
Do cap body copy at 65-75ch and get to the point fast — assume a reader with under two minutes per case study.
Do show problem complexity (a framework visual, decision matrix, or system diagram) before showing UI screens in a case study.
Do hold the creative-coding/side-quest components to the same craft bar as case study components — they are proof of range, not filler.
donts: Don't use border-left/border-right stripes greater than 1px as a colored accent on cards or callouts.
Don't use gradient text for emphasis — use ink weight or size instead.
Don't default to glassmorphism; the homepage nav's 15% white tint is deliberate and singular, not a pattern to repeat elsewhere.
Don't build a generic hero-metric block (big number, small label, gradient accent) — it reads as SaaS template, not portfolio craft.
Don't repeat identical icon-plus-heading card grids; vary card treatment by what the content actually needs.
Don't reach for a modal as the first solution — favor inline and progressive disclosure.
Don't mix two borrowed project colors on the same screen.
Don't let a case study run long and text-heavy.
Don't visually frame AI/conversational work with cute chat-bubble or chatbot iconography.
Don't add a shadow, gradient, or decorative flourish to compensate for thin content.
